actions.name: get_alert
actions.description: Get one alert workflow in a Sentry organization by workflow id.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the alert workflow.

alertId.description: The alert workflow id to retrieve.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one Sentry alert workflow.

actions.name: get_issue
actions.description: Get one issue in a Sentry organization by numeric id or short id.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the issue.

issueId.description: The Sentry issue id or short id to retrieve.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one Sentry issue.

issue.description: A Sentry issue summary or detail payload.

actions.name: get_issue_event
actions.description: Get one event for a Sentry issue by event id, or use latest, oldest, or recommended selectors.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the issue.

issueId.description: The Sentry issue id whose event should be retrieved.

eventId.description: The event id or selector such as latest, oldest, or recommended.

environments.description: The environment names used to filter which issue event Sentry selects.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one event from a Sentry issue.

event.description: A normalized event associated with a Sentry issue.

actions.name: get_organization_integration
actions.description: Get one installed Sentry organization integration by its integration id.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry organization id or slug that owns the integration.

integrationId.description: The installed integration id to retrieve.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving an installed Sentry integration.

integration.description: An installed integration within a Sentry organization.

actions.name: get_organization_integration_config
actions.description: List available integration provider configs for a Sentry organization, optionally filtered by provider key.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry organization id or slug whose integration config should be retrieved.

providerKey.description: Optional provider key filter such as slack, github, or jira.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving Sentry integration provider config.

providers.description: The integration provider configs returned by Sentry.

actions.name: get_organization_release
actions.description: Get one release in a Sentry organization, with optional health and summary statistics included.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the release.

version.description: The Sentry release version identifier to retrieve.

health.description: Whether Sentry should include release health details in the response.

summaryStatsPeriod.description: The relative time period used for release summary statistics.

healthStatsPeriod.description: The relative time period used for release health statistics.

adoptionStages.description: Whether Sentry should include release adoption stage information.

projectId.description: An optional project id used to scope the release details.

query.description: An optional Sentry query string used to filter release statistics.

sort.description: The sort field used for release statistics in the Sentry response.

status.description: An optional release status filter such as open or archived.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one Sentry release.

actions.name: get_project
actions.description: Get one Sentry project by organization and project slug or id.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the project.

projectIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry project id or slug to retrieve.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one Sentry project.

project.description: A Sentry project returned by project list or detail endpoints.

actions.name: get_release_health_stats
actions.description: Retrieve release health session statistics for one Sentry release by querying the sessions endpoint with that release version.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the release.

version.description: The Sentry release version to filter release health statistics by.

fields.description: The session metric fields that Sentry should calculate.

groupBy.description: The session dimensions that Sentry should group by.

query.description: An optional additional Sentry search query appended to the release filter.

start.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 start time used to query release health data.

end.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 end time used to query release health data.

environments.description: The environment names used to filter release health statistics.

projectIds.description: The numeric Sentry project ids used to filter release health statistics.

interval.description: The interval used for time-series release health statistics.

statsPeriod.description: The relative time period such as 24h or 7d used for release health statistics.

includeSeries.description: Whether Sentry should include series data, using 1 for yes and 0 for no.

includeTotals.description: Whether Sentry should include totals data, using 1 for yes and 0 for no.

perPage.description: The maximum number of grouped rows to return.

orderBy.description: The metric field Sentry should order the grouped rows by.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving Sentry release health statistics.

items.description: A grouped release health statistics row.

groups.description: The grouped release health statistics returned by Sentry.

intervals.description: The interval boundaries returned by the Sentry sessions endpoint.

anyOf.description: The ISO 8601 start time applied to the statistics query.
The ISO 8601 end time applied to the statistics query.

actions.name: get_replay
actions.description: Get one replay instance in a Sentry organization by replay id.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the replay.

start.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 start time used to scope replay detail metrics.

end.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 end time used to scope replay detail metrics.

statsPeriod.description: The relative time period such as 1h or 7d used to scope replay detail metrics.

sort.description: The replay detail sort field returned by Sentry, optionally prefixed with -.

field.description: Additional replay detail fields that Sentry should include in the response.

query.description: The Sentry replay search query string used to scope the replay detail response.

cursor.description: The opaque Sentry pagination cursor for nested replay detail data.

projectIds.description: The numeric Sentry project ids used to scope replay details.

perPage.description: The maximum number of nested replay detail rows to return.

environment.description: The environment name used to scope replay details.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving one Sentry replay.

anyOf.description: The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the next page, or null when there are no more results.
The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the previous page, or null when there are no earlier results.

actions.name: get_sentry_app
actions.description: Get one Sentry App by id or slug, including integration metadata and OAuth client settings.

sentryAppIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry App id or slug to retrieve from the global Sentry App registry.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for retrieving a Sentry App.

sentryApp.description: A Sentry App with integration and OAuth settings details.

actions.name: list_alerts
actions.description: List alert workflows for a Sentry organization, with optional id, project, and search filters.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug whose alert workflows should be listed.

ids.description: Specific alert workflow ids that Sentry should filter the list to.

query.description: An optional free-text search query used to filter alert workflows.

sortBy.description: The alert workflow sort field, optionally prefixed with - for descending.

projectIds.description: The numeric Sentry project ids used to filter alert workflows.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing Sentry alert workflows.

alerts.description: The alert workflows returned by Sentry.

actions.name: list_issue_events
actions.description: List events that belong to one Sentry issue, with optional event query filters.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the issue.

issueId.description: The Sentry issue id whose events should be listed.

full.description: Whether Sentry should return full event payloads instead of summaries.

sample.description: Whether Sentry should return a deterministic sample of issue events.

query.description: The Sentry event search query string used to filter issue events.

start.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 start time used to filter issue events.

end.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 end time used to filter issue events.

environments.description: The environment names used to filter issue events.

statsPeriod.description: The relative stats period such as 24h or 7d used to filter issue events.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing events attached to one Sentry issue.

items.description: A normalized event associated with a Sentry issue.

events.description: The issue events returned by Sentry.

actions.name: list_organization_integrations
actions.description: List installed integrations for a Sentry organization, with optional provider and feature filters.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry organization id or slug whose installed integrations should be listed.

providerKey.description: Optional provider key filter such as slack, github, or jira.

includeConfig.description: Whether to ask Sentry to include expanded third-party configuration details.

features.description: Optional provider feature filters such as alert-rule or issue-sync.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing installed Sentry integrations.

items.description: An installed integration within a Sentry organization.

integrations.description: The installed integrations returned for the organization.

actions.name: list_organization_issues
actions.description: List issues for a Sentry organization with optional search, project, and environment filters.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug whose issues should be listed.

query.description: The Sentry issue search query string used to filter the results.

sort.description: The issue sort order such as date, freq, inbox, new, trends, or user.

limit.description: The maximum number of issues to return in one page.

start.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 start time used to filter the issue results.

end.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 end time used to filter the issue results.

cursor.description: The opaque Sentry pagination cursor for the issue results.

expand.description: Additional issue data keys that Sentry should expand in the response.

collapse.description: Response sections that Sentry should collapse or omit from the payload.

environments.description: The environment names used to filter issues.

projectIds.description: The numeric Sentry project ids used to filter issues.

statsPeriod.description: The relative stats period such as 24h or 7d used for issue statistics.

shortIdLookup.description: Whether Sentry should parse short issue ids inside the query string.

groupStatsPeriod.description: The issue group statistics window such as auto, 24h, or 14d.

viewId.description: The Sentry saved view id whose filters should be applied.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing Sentry organization issues.

items.description: A Sentry issue summary or detail payload.

anyOf.description: The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the next page, or null when there are no more results.
The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the previous page, or null when there are no earlier results.

actions.name: list_organization_projects
actions.description: List projects that belong to a Sentry organization.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry organization id or slug whose projects should be listed.

cursor.description: The opaque Sentry pagination cursor for the next or previous page.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing Sentry organization projects.

items.description: A Sentry project returned by project list or detail endpoints.

anyOf.description: The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the next page, or null when there are no more results.
The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the previous page, or null when there are no earlier results.

actions.name: list_organization_releases
actions.description: List releases that belong to a Sentry organization, optionally filtered by version prefix.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug whose releases should be listed.

query.description: An optional release version prefix used to filter the release list.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing Sentry organization releases.

actions.name: list_organization_replays
actions.description: List session replays for a Sentry organization, with optional project and environment filters.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug whose replays should be listed.

start.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 start time used to filter replays.

end.description: The inclusive ISO 8601 end time used to filter replays.

statsPeriod.description: The relative time period such as 1d or 7d used to filter replays.

sort.description: The replay sort field returned by Sentry, optionally prefixed with -.

field.description: Additional replay fields that Sentry should include in each result.

query.description: The Sentry replay search query string used to filter results.

cursor.description: The opaque Sentry pagination cursor for the replay results.

projectIds.description: The numeric Sentry project ids used to filter replays.

perPage.description: The maximum number of replay rows to return.

environment.description: The environment name used to filter replays.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing Sentry organization replays.

anyOf.description: The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the next page, or null when there are no more results.
The opaque Sentry cursor from the Link header for the previous page, or null when there are no earlier results.

actions.name: list_organization_sentry_apps
actions.description: List the custom Sentry Apps created by a Sentry organization.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The Sentry organization id or slug whose custom Sentry Apps should be listed.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for listing organization-owned Sentry Apps.

items.description: A Sentry App with integration and OAuth settings details.

sentryApps.description: The custom Sentry Apps returned for the organization.

actions.name: update_issue
actions.description: Update mutable attributes on one Sentry issue, such as status, assignment, or bookmarks.

organizationIdOrSlug.description: The organization id or slug that owns the issue.

issueId.description: The Sentry issue id to update.

status.description: The new issue status such as resolved, resolvedInNextRelease, unresolved, or ignored.

hasSeen.description: Whether the current user has seen the issue after this update.

isPublic.description: Whether the issue should be visible via a public permalink.

assignedTo.description: The assignee actor id, username, or email address; use an empty string to unassign.

isBookmarked.description: Whether the current user should bookmark the issue after this update.

isSubscribed.description: Whether the current user should subscribe to issue notifications.

inCommit.description: The commit hash associated with the issue resolution.

inRelease.description: The release version in which the issue is considered resolved.

inNextRelease.description: Whether the issue is considered resolved in the next release.

statusDetails.description: Additional issue resolution details sent to Sentry.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for updating one Sentry issue.

issue.description: A Sentry issue summary or detail payload.
